rulings.Rulings: Denied without prejudice to renewal or subsequent motion. Subsequent to this motion being filed on the basis of an affirmative defense to the First Amended Petition, Petitioner filed his Second Amended Petition, which--at least unless struck or dismissed--stands as the operative pleading. "[O]nce an amended complaint is filed, it is error to grant summary adjudication on a cause of action contained in a previous complaint." State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-31 (reversing grant of summary judgment on statute of limitations affirmative defense). "After a cause of action is amended, the court may rule in favor of the defendant if, upon subsequent motion, or perhaps renewal of the earlier motion if appropriately framed, it is shown . . . there are no triable material issues of fact which would permit recovery on that theory." (Id. emphasis deleted.) Even assuming the bona fide purchaser affirmative defense remains applicable to the amended Probate Code section 850 cause of action, the Court cannot reach the issue on this motion given the current procedural posture of the case. Nothing in Wang v. Nibbelink (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 1 affects or questions the holding of State Compensation Ins. Fund that a motion for summary judgment cannot be granted as to a superceded complaint, even though the motion seeks judgment on the basis of an affirmative defense arguably still applicable after the amendment. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, March 4, 2020.
